claims.text: The variant NM_000152.5:c.1935C>A (p.Asp645Glu) is identified in the paper with ClinVar ID VCV000004029.

In the Mini-COMET Cohort 1 overall population, c.1935C>A appears in cis with c.1726G>A on one allele in at least two patients, as compound heterozygous genotypes: c.[784G>A];[1935C>A;1726G>A] and c.[1062C>G;1286A>G];[1935C>A;1726G>A].

In the Mini-COMET Cohort 2 overall population, c.1935C>A appears in combination with c.1726G>A in multiple patients, including at least one patient who appears homozygous for the [1935C>A;1726G>A] haplotype and one compound heterozygous patient with [1935C>A;1726G>A];[2842INST].
The c.1935C>A variant is found in cis with c.1726G>A (p.Gly576Ser, ClinVar ID VCV000092467) across multiple patients in both Cohort 1 and Cohort 2 of the Mini-COMET trial.

citations.quote: NM_000152.5:c.1935C > A (p.Asp645Glu) (ClinVar ID VCV000004029); NM_000152.5:c.1726G > A (p.Gly576Ser) (ClinVar ID VCV000092467)

claims.text: The patients carrying c.1935C>A in the Mini-COMET trial had IOPD and had previously received alglucosidase alfa, demonstrating clinical decline or suboptimal response before enrolling in the trial.

citations.quote: Mini-COMET is a Phase 2, multi-stage, randomized, open-label, multicenter, multinational, ascending-dose cohort trial primarily evaluating the safety of avalglucosidase alfa in patients with IOPD who previously treated with a stable dose of alglucosidase alfa. Patients who showed clinical decline with alglucosidase alfa treatment classified as stage 1 (Cohort 1 and 2), and patients who showed suboptimal clinical response with alglucosidase alfa treatment classified as stage 2 (Cohort 3)
